summary_strategy: Reframe summary around building deployable detection systems and SOC-enabling automation, emphasizing operational impact (MTTR reduction, false positive reduction) over theoretical security concepts

skills_tailoring.Languages: Reorder to prioritize Python, PowerShell, Bash, Go, and Rust first (most relevant for SOC automation and tooling), followed by Java, C, C++, TypeScript, SQL (for internal tooling and data pipelines), then Swift (least relevant for this role)
skills_tailoring.Security Tooling: Reorder to highlight CrowdStrike Falcon, Defender XDR, SIEM/EDR/XDR/SOAR first; add 'CrowdStrike RTR' explicitly as a distinct item since it's used in Great Wolf experience; group endpoint management and vulnerability management together under operational tooling
skills_tailoring.AI/ML and Research: Rename to 'Security AI & Analytics' only if absolutely required — but per instructions, must keep exact name 'AI/ML and Research'; reorder to surface RAG pipelines, LangChain, vector databases first; add 'embeddings-based retrieval' and 'retrieval tuning' as explicit items from inventory
skills_tailoring.Frameworks and Infrastructure: Reorder to prioritize Flask, FastAPI, Docker first (for internal tooling and containerized security services); add 'MCP/FastMCP' explicitly; keep React.js/Next.js but deprioritize behind backend frameworks
skills_tailoring.DevOps and CI/CD: Reorder to emphasize AWS/GCP/Azure, container orchestration (ECS), secrets management first; add 'Terraform', 'Ansible' explicitly from inventory; keep CI/CD pipelines but reorder after cloud-native foundations
skills_tailoring.Databases: Reorder to prioritize PostgreSQL, Snowflake, vector databases first (for log analytics and RAG); keep all 10 items; add 'Databricks' before ClickHouse if vector work is emphasized

experience_focus.company: University of California, Office of the President
experience_focus.must_highlight: Coraline multi-source ingestion and confidence-matching (directly maps to SIEM/EDR correlation logic), AI/ML-powered RAG chatbot (for alert enrichment and SOC knowledge retrieval), vulnerability response automation for macOS/Windows fleets
experience_focus.claims_to_avoid: Do not claim SOC on-call rotation or incident response ownership unless explicitly stated; avoid 'led' for cross-functional work — use 'collaborated with IAM/Networking teams to implement'

experience_focus.must_highlight: CrowdStrike RTR PowerShell tooling (endpoint remediation), Microsoft Graph API automation for distribution lists, Pandas/NumPy log analysis in Rapid7 (anomaly detection), KnowBe4 phishing analysis with 25% compliance lift
experience_focus.safe_metrics_to_keep: 10,000+ devices, 200 hours saved annually, 500 hours reduced workload
experience_focus.claims_to_avoid: Do not claim 'SOC escalation point' or 'incident response leadership'; avoid conflating Great Wolf support role with detection engineering responsibilities

experience_focus.must_highlight: CI/CD pipeline security, Selenium accessibility testing (WCAG), cross-browser test automation reducing user-reported issues
experience_focus.claims_to_avoid: Do not overstate security scope — frame as 'secure releases' and 'compliance-by-default', not detection engineering

risk_controls: Never invent metrics beyond those explicitly stated (e.g., no 'improved detection accuracy by X%'),
Never add responsibilities not supported in baseline resume (e.g., no SOC on-call, no people management),
Replace vague phrases like 'leveraged' or 'synergized' with concrete verbs: built, engineered, automated, integrated, tuned
Do not rename or merge skill categories — preserve exact section headers and item counts
